channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: These rock-climbing fish can shimmy up a 50-foot waterfall
item.description: New research from the Democratic Republic of Congo offers a behavioral and anatomical portrait of a species that can achieve surprising athletic feats.
item.content:encoded:
New research from the Democratic Republic of Congo offers a behavioral and anatomical portrait of a species that can achieve surprising athletic feats.

(Image credit: Pacifique Kiwele Mutambala)
item.dc:creator: Ari Daniel

item.title: In Lebanon, more than 50 medics have been killed by Israel. Some say they're targeted
item.description: Lebanon says at least 54 health workers are among more than 1,400 people killed by Israel during the current invasion. Human rights groups say first responders are being targeted — something Israel denies.
item.content:encoded:
Lebanon says at least 54 health workers are among more than 1,400 people killed by Israel during the current invasion. Human rights groups say first responders are being targeted — something Israel denies.

(Image credit: Claire Harbage)
item.dc:creator: Lauren Frayer

item.title: Questions to help you get 'financially naked' with your partner
item.description: Having "brutally honest conversations" about money can bring couples closer together, says Vivian Tu, a financial educator. She shares questions to ask your partner at every relationship stage.
item.content:encoded: Having "brutally honest conversations" about money can bring couples closer together, says Vivian Tu, a financial educator. She shares questions to ask your partner at every relationship stage.
item.dc:creator: Marielle Segarra

item.title: More teens are getting hooked on gambling. Parents say it often goes undetected
item.description: The explosion of online gambling and sports betting, as well as the advertising behind it, is attracting a growing number of young people, most of them boys.
item.content:encoded: The explosion of online gambling and sports betting, as well as the advertising behind it, is attracting a growing number of young people, most of them boys.
item.dc:creator: Sequoia Carrillo

item.title: Trump says U.S. military has rescued airman shot down over Iran
item.description: A U.S. Airforce officer whose plane was shot down in Iran was rescued by U.S. forces early Sunday after evading capture for more than a day in enemy territory, President Trump announced on social media.
item.content:encoded:
A U.S. Airforce officer whose plane was shot down in Iran was rescued by U.S. forces early Sunday after evading capture for more than a day in enemy territory, President Trump announced on social media.

(Image credit: Alex Brandon)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: German researchers set right the story of a 9,000-year-old shaman's grave
item.description: When a 9,000 year-old grave of a shaman was discovered in Nazi Germany, the discovery was quickly politicized to support Nazi propaganda. But new analysis shows that initial narrative was all wrong.
item.content:encoded: When a 9,000 year-old grave of a shaman was discovered in Nazi Germany, the discovery was quickly politicized to support Nazi propaganda. But new analysis shows that initial narrative was all wrong.
item.dc:creator: Rob Schmitz

item.title: Judge halts Trump effort requiring colleges to show they don't consider race in admissions
item.description: A federal judge on Saturday said the Trump Administration the demand to collect data from universities was rolled out in a "rushed and chaotic" manner.
item.content:encoded:
A federal judge on Saturday said the Trump Administration the demand to collect data from universities was rolled out in a "rushed and chaotic" manner.

(Image credit: Alex Brandon)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: After the Minnesota surge, ICE is moving to a quieter enforcement approach
item.description: ICE seems to be changing from aggressive immigration enforcement on city streets to an apparent return to operations that rely heavily on local law enforcement. But even in Florida, where sheriffs are required to cooperate with ICE, some conservative sheriffs have concerns about pursuing immigrants with no criminal records.
item.content:encoded:
ICE seems to be changing from aggressive immigration enforcement on city streets to an apparent return to operations that rely heavily on local law enforcement. But even in Florida, where sheriffs are required to cooperate with ICE, some conservative sheriffs have concerns about pursuing immigrants with no criminal records.

(Image credit: Joe Raedle)
item.dc:creator: Meg Anderson

item.title: 'London Falling': A teenage imposter, an aging gangster and a body in the Thames
item.description: In 2019, 19-year-old Zac Brettler leapt towards the River Thames from a fifth-floor luxury apartment in central London. Patrick Radden Keefe investigates the story of the teen's double life in a new book.
item.content:encoded: In 2019, 19-year-old Zac Brettler leapt towards the River Thames from a fifth-floor luxury apartment in central London. Patrick Radden Keefe investigates the story of the teen's double life in a new book.
item.dc:creator: Frank Langfitt

item.title: Opinion: Humanity's hopes ascended with Artemis II
item.description: NPR's Scott Simon reflects on the successful launch of NASA's Artemis II this week. The four astronauts aboard will travel around the moon.
item.content:encoded:
NPR's Scott Simon reflects on the successful launch of NASA's Artemis II this week. The four astronauts aboard will travel around the moon.

(Image credit: Chris O'Meara)
item.dc:creator: Scott Simon
